children.label: A
children.text: stating that the order, any provision of the order, or any obligation imposed in connection with the order, is not established in accordance with law; and

children.label: B
children.text: requesting a modification of the order or an exemption from the order.

children.heading: Hearing
children.label: 2 Hearing
children.text: The Secretary shall give the petitioner an opportunity for a hearing on the petition, in accordance with regulations issued by the Secretary.

children.heading: Ruling
children.label: 3 Ruling
children.text: After the hearing, the Secretary shall make a ruling on the petition. The ruling shall be final, subject to review as set forth in subsection (b).

children.heading: Limitation on petition
children.label: 4 Limitation on petition
children.text: Any petition filed under this subsection challenging an order, any provision of the order, or any obligation imposed in connection with the order, shall be filed not less than 2 years after the effective date of the order, provision, or obligation subject to challenge in the petition.

children.heading: Commencement of action
children.label: 1 Commencement of action
children.text: The district courts of the United States in any district in which a person who is a petitioner under subsection (a) resides or conducts business shall have jurisdiction to review the ruling of the Secretary on the petition of the person, if a complaint requesting the review is filed no later than 30 days after the date of the entry of the ruling by the Secretary.

children.heading: Process
children.label: 2 Process
children.text: Service of process in proceedings under this subsection shall be conducted in accordance with the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

children.label: A
children.text: to make such ruling as the court shall determine to be in accordance with law; or

children.label: B
children.text: to take such further action as, in the opinion of the court, the law requires.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: c Enforcement
children.text: The pendency of proceedings instituted under this section shall not impede, hinder, or delay the Attorney General or the Secretary from obtaining relief under .section 8708 of this title
